name: Bard
description: An inspiring magician whose power echoes the music of creation, weaving words, song, and dance into spells and abilities that lift allies and confound foes.

features.name: Proficiencies
features.description: Your Bard training grants the following proficiencies.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration
features.description:
You can supernaturally inspire others through words, music, or dance. This inspiration is represented by your Bardic Inspiration die, which is a d6.

**Using Bardic Inspiration.** As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can inspire another creature within 60 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains one of your Bardic Inspiration dice. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time.

Once within the next hour when the creature fails a {rule:D20 Test}, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning the failure into a success. A Bardic Inspiration die is expended when it's rolled.

**Number of Uses.** You can confer a Bardic Inspiration die a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**At Higher Levels.** Your Bardic Inspiration die changes when you reach certain Bard levels, as shown in the Bardic Die column of the Bard Features table. The die becomes a d8 at level 5, a d10 at level 10, and a d12 at level 15.

properties.text: Bardic Inspiration die: d6 (levels 1-4), d8 (levels 5-9), d10 (levels 10-14), d12 (levels 15-20). The inspired creature can roll the die and add it to a D20 Test after failing, within 1 hour.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description:
You have learned to cast spells through your bardic arts. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Bard spells.

**Cantrips.** You know two cantrips of your choice from the Bard spell list. Dancing Lights and Vicious Mockery are recommended. When you reach Bard levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice.

**Spell Slots.** The Bard Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**Prepared Spells of Level 1+.** You prepare the list of level 1+ spells available for you to cast. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Bard levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the Bard spell list. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

**Changing Your Prepared Spells.** Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Bard spell for which you have spell slots.

**Spellcasting Focus.** You can use a Musical Instrument as a {rule:Spellcasting Focus|Spellcasting Focus} for your Bard spells.

features.name: Expertise
features.description:
You gain {rule:Expertise|Expertise} in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Performance and Persuasion are recommended if you have proficiency in them.

At Bard level 9, you gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Jack of All Trades
features.description:
You can add half your {rule:Proficiency|Proficiency Bonus} (round down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack and that doesn't otherwise use your Proficiency Bonus.

For example, if you make a Strength (Athletics) check and lack Athletics proficiency, you can add half your Proficiency Bonus to the check.

properties.text: Add half Proficiency Bonus (rounded down) to any ability check using a skill you lack proficiency in, provided Proficiency Bonus isn't already applied.

features.name: Bard Subclass
features.description: You gain a Bard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Bard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Bard level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Bard levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Font of Inspiration
features.description:
You now regain all your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}.

In addition, you can expend a spell slot (no action required) to regain one expended use of Bardic Inspiration.

properties.text: Bardic Inspiration now resets on Short Rest as well as Long Rest. Can also expend a spell slot (no action) to recover 1 use.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Bard Subclass.

features.name: Countercharm
features.description: You can use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the {condition:Charmed} or {condition:Frightened} condition, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to cause the save to be rerolled, and the new roll has {rule:Advantage}.

properties.text: Reaction: when you or a creature within 30 ft fails a save against Charmed or Frightened, you can cause the save to be rerolled with Advantage.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain {rule:Expertise|Expertise} in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: You've learned secrets from various magical traditions. Whenever you reach a Bard level (including this level) and the Prepared Spells number in the Bard Features table increases, you can choose any of your new prepared spells from the Bard, Cleric, Druid, and Wizard spell lists, and the chosen spells count as Bard spells for you. In addition, whenever you replace a spell prepared for this class, you can replace it with a spell from those lists.

properties.text: When your Prepared Spells count increases, new spells can be chosen from Bard, Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell lists. Replacement spells can also come from those lists.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Bard Subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Superior Inspiration
features.description: When you roll {rule:Initiative}, you regain expended uses of Bardic Inspiration until you have two if you have fewer than that.

properties.text: On Initiative roll, if you have fewer than 2 Bardic Inspiration uses, you regain uses until you have 2.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Spell Recall is recommended.

features.name: Words of Creation
features.description: You have mastered two of the Words of Creation: the words of life and death. You therefore always have the {spell:Power Word Heal} and {spell:Power Word Kill} spells prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within 10 feet of the first target.

properties.text: Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill are always prepared. When casting either, you can target a second creature within 10 feet of the first target.

subclasses.name: College of Dance
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Dance know that the Words of Creation can't be contained within speech or song; the words are uttered by the movements of celestial bodies and flow through the motions of the smallest creatures. These Bards practice a way of being in harmony with the whirling cosmos that emphasizes agility, speed, and grace.

features.name: Dazzling Footwork
features.description:
While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the following benefits.

**Dance Virtuoso.** You have {rule:Advantage} on any Charisma (Performance) check you make that involves you dancing.

**Unarmored Defense.** Your base {rule:Armor Class} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers.

**Agile Strikes.** When you expend a use of your Bardic Inspiration as part of an action, a {rule:Bonus Action}, or a {rule:Reaction}, you can make one {rule:Unarmed Strike|Unarmed Strike} as part of that action, Bonus Action, or Reaction.

**Bardic Damage.** You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack rolls of your Unarmed Strikes. When you deal damage with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die plus your Dexterity modifier, instead of the strike's normal damage. This roll doesn't expend the die.

modifiers.condition: while not wearing armor and not wielding a Shield

properties.text: All benefits apply only while not wearing armor or wielding a Shield.
Agile Strikes: when you expend a Bardic Inspiration use as part of an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction, you can make one Unarmed Strike as part of that same action.
Bardic Damage: can use DEX for Unarmed Strike attack rolls. On hit, can deal Bludgeoning damage = Bardic Inspiration die roll + DEX modifier (die not expended).

features.name: Inspiring Movement
features.description:
When an enemy you can see ends its turn within 5 feet of you, you can take a {rule:Reaction} and expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to move up to half your {rule:Speed}. Then one ally of your choice within 30 feet of you can also move up to half their Speed using their Reaction.

None of this feature's movement provokes {action:Opportunity Attack|Opportunity Attacks}.

properties.text: Reaction + 1 Bardic Inspiration: when an enemy within 5 ft ends its turn, move up to half Speed (no Opportunity Attacks). One ally within 30 ft can also use their Reaction to move up to half their Speed (no Opportunity Attacks).

features.name: Tandem Footwork
features.description: When you roll {rule:Initiative}, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration if you don't have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition. When you do so, roll your Bardic Inspiration die; you and each ally within 30 feet of you who can see or hear you gains a bonus to Initiative equal to the number rolled.

properties.text: On Initiative roll (if not Incapacitated), expend 1 Bardic Inspiration: roll the die and add the result as a bonus to Initiative for you and all allies within 30 ft who can see or hear you.

features.name: Leading Evasion
features.description:
When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. If any creatures within 5 feet of you are making the same Dexterity saving throw, you can share this benefit with them for that save.

You can't use this feature if you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition.

properties.text: Creatures within 5 ft of you gain the benefit of this feature against the same effect.

subclasses.name: College of Glamour
subclasses.description: The College of Glamour traces its origins to the beguiling magic of the Feywild. Bards who study this magic weave threads of beauty and terror into their songs and stories, and the mightiest among them can cloak themselves in otherworldly majesty. Their performances stir up wistful longing for forgotten innocence, evoke unconscious memories of long-held fears, and tug at the emotions of even the most hard-hearted listeners.

features.name: Beguiling Magic
features.description:
You always have the {spell:Charm Person} and {spell:Mirror Image} spells prepared.

In addition, immediately after you cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a spell slot, you can cause a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the {condition:Charmed} or {condition:Frightened} condition (your choice) for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also restore your use of it by expending one use of your Bardic Inspiration (no action required).

properties.text: After casting an Enchantment or Illusion spell with a slot, trigger Beguiling Magic: one creature within 60 ft makes WIS save or gains Charmed or Frightened (your choice) for 1 minute (repeating save each turn). Restore this use by expending 1 Bardic Inspiration (no action).

features.name: Mantle of Inspiration
features.description: You can weave fey magic into a song or dance to fill others with vigor. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can expend a use of Bardic Inspiration, rolling a Bardic Inspiration die. When you do so, choose a number of other creatures within 60 feet of yourself, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of those creatures gains a number of {rule:Temporary Hit Points|Temporary Hit Points} equal to two times the number rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die, and then each can use its {rule:Reaction} to move up to its {rule:Speed} without provoking {action:Opportunity Attack|Opportunity Attacks}.

properties.text: Up to CHA modifier creatures (min 1) within 60 ft each gain Temporary HP equal to 2 × the Bardic Inspiration die result, and can Reaction-move up to their Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

features.name: Mantle of Majesty
features.description:
You always have the {spell:Command} spell prepared.

As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you cast {spell:Command} without expending a spell slot, and you take on an unearthly appearance for 1 minute or until your {status:Concentration} ends. During this time, you can cast {spell:Command} as a Bonus Action without expending a spell slot.

Any creature {condition:Charmed} by you automatically fails its saving throw against the {spell:Command} you cast with this feature.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 3+ spell slot (no action required).

properties.text: Bonus Action: cast Command without a spell slot and assume unearthly appearance for 1 min (Concentration). Each turn of the duration, cast Command again as Bonus Action (no slot). Charmed creatures auto-fail saves against this Command. Restore use by expending a level 3+ spell slot (no action).

features.name: Unbreakable Majesty
features.description:
As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can assume a magically majestic presence for 1 minute or until you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition. For the duration, whenever any creature hits you with an attack roll for the first time on a turn, the attacker must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, or the attack misses instead, as the creature recoils from your majesty.

Once you assume this majestic presence, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Short Rest} or {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Bonus Action: assume majestic presence for 1 min (ends if Incapacitated). The first time each turn a creature hits you with an attack roll, it must make a CHA save (DC = your spell save DC) or the attack misses.

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Lore collect spells and secrets from diverse sources, such as scholarly tomes, mystical rites, and peasant tales. The college's members gather in libraries and universities to share their lore with one another. They also meet at festivals or affairs of state, where they can expose corruption, unravel lies, and poke fun at self-important figures of authority.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: You learn to use your wit to supernaturally distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself makes a damage roll or succeeds on an ability check or attack roll, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration; roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and subtract the number rolled from the creature's roll, reducing the damage or potentially turning the success into a failure.

properties.text: Reaction + 1 Bardic Inspiration: when a creature within 60 ft makes a damage roll or succeeds on an ability check or attack roll, subtract the Bardic Inspiration die result from the roll (potentially turning success into failure).

features.name: Magical Discoveries
features.description:
You learn two spells of your choice. These spells can come from the Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list or any combination thereof. A spell you choose must be a cantrip or a spell for which you have spell slots, as shown in the Bard Features table.

You always have the chosen spells prepared, and whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one of the spells with another spell that meets these requirements.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.description: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration; roll the Bardic Inspiration die, and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning a failure into a success. On a failure, the Bardic Inspiration isn't expended.

properties.text: After failing an ability check or attack roll, expend 1 Bardic Inspiration: roll the die and add it to the d20, potentially turning failure into success. If still a failure, the Bardic Inspiration is not expended.

subclasses.name: College of Valor
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring storytellers whose tales preserve the memory of the great heroes of the past. These Bards sing the deeds of the mighty in vaulted halls or to crowds gathered around great bonfires. They travel to witness great events firsthand and to ensure that the memory of these events doesn't pass away. With their songs, they inspire new generations to reach the same heights of accomplishment as the heroes of old.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.description:
You can use your wit to turn the tide of battle. A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it for one of the following effects.

**Defense.** When the creature is hit by an attack roll, that creature can use its {rule:Reaction} to roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to its AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.

**Offense.** Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the attack's damage against the target.

properties.text: Creatures with your Bardic Inspiration die can use it for: Defense (Reaction when hit — add die result to AC against that attack) or Offense (after hitting — add die result to the attack's damage).

features.name: Martial Training
features.description:
You gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor and Shields.

In addition, you can use a Simple or Martial weapon as a {rule:Spellcasting Focus|Spellcasting Focus} to cast spells from your Bard spell list.

properties.text: Can use a Simple or Martial weapon as a Spellcasting Focus for Bard spells.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description:
You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {action:Attack} action on your turn.

In addition, you can cast one of your cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

properties.text: One of the two attacks from Extra Attack can be replaced with casting an action-time cantrip.

features.name: Battle Magic
features.description: After you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can make one attack with a weapon as a {rule:Bonus Action}.

properties.text: After casting a spell with a casting time of 1 action, you can make one weapon attack as a Bonus Action.
